HMMER License
==============

Tool Name: HMMER (profile hidden Markov models for protein sequence analysis)
License Type: BSD 3-Clause License
Copyright Holder: Howard Hughes Medical Institute
Author: Sean R. Eddy
Official Website: http://hmmer.org/

License Features
---------------

The BSD 3-Clause license is a permissive open-source license that allows:
- Commercial use
- Modification
- Distribution
- Private use

The only obligations are:
- Preserve copyright notice
- Preserve license text
- Do not use copyright holder's name for promotion

Included Tools
--------------

HMMER tool suite components included in TBtools:
- hmmpress.exe - Create HMM database indexes
- hmmsearch.exe - Use HMM to search sequence databases

Citation Information
--------------------

When using HMMER in academic research, please cite the following literature:

Eddy SR. (2011)
"Accelerated profile HMM searches."
PLoS Comput Biol 7(10):e1002195.

And the original HMMER methodology paper:

Eddy SR. (1998)
"Profile hidden Markov models."
Bioinformatics 14(9):755-763.

Technical Notes
---------------

HMMER is software for biological sequence analysis, particularly suited for:
- Protein family detection
- Sequence homology search
- Protein domain identification
- Sequence annotation and classification

HMMER uses hidden Markov model (HMM) technology, which provides higher sensitivity and specificity than traditional methods.

----------

This software is provided "as is" without any express or implied warranty. Howard Hughes Medical Institute assumes no liability for any damages resulting from the use of this software.
